title: Power Integrations Earnings Call Shows Momentum Building
text: Power Integrations reported encouraging Q1 results with revenue of $108.3 million, up 3% year over year and 5% sequentially, signaling early recovery from a cyclical downturn. Financial Highlights: Non-GAAP gross margin improved to 53.5%. Operating margin reached 11.7%, up 200 basis points. Net income of $13.9 million, or $0.25 per diluted share. Generated $20 million in operating cash flow. Growth Drivers: Industrial was the standout segment, with revenue climbing 23% year-over-year. The company is gaining traction in automotive, with engagement involving 17 of the top 20 EV automakers and expectations to double automotive revenue this year. Forward Guidance: Management provided Q2 guidance of $115-$120 million in revenue with gross margin expansion to 54%-55% and operating margin of 13.5%-15.5%. Strategic Opportunities: The company highlighted long-term potential in data-center applications through collaboration with NVIDIA on high-voltage GaN devices, though major revenue contributions are expected to be multi-year in nature.
